widget.body: A LinkedIn post became a professional industry analysis in under an hour.

itemInlines.text: and the strategic assumptions hidden inside the ranking

widget.body:
$94.3bnCAPEX represented in the original visual
17Independent sources consulted
4Amounts independently verified as development CAPEX

entries.text: The input was a LinkedIn post. The output was an industry perspective. In under an hour, a simple ranking of the largest FPSO projects became a structured examination of project scope, CAPEX comparability, development status, omitted projects, ownership models, phased versus integrated development, and the strategic assumptions hidden inside the ranking.

widget.body: The input was a LinkedIn post. The output was an industry perspective.

heading.text: Giacomo Prandelli’s post makes a sharp geographic argument: nine of ten highest-CAPEX FPSO projects shown sit in the South Atlantic.

inlines.text: Giacomo Prandelli’s post makes a sharp geographic argument: nine of ten highest-CAPEX FPSO projects shown sit in the South Atlantic.

paragraph.text: Scan to open the original LinkedIn post and visual.

inlines.text: Scan to open the original LinkedIn post and visual.

paragraph.text: Uaru and Whiptail lead at US$12.7bn each. Petrobras supplies five entries. Only Bonga Southwest/Aparo sits outside the South Atlantic cluster.

inlines.text: Uaru and Whiptail lead at US$12.7bn each. Petrobras supplies five entries. Only Bonga Southwest/Aparo sits outside the South Atlantic cluster.

entries.text: We begin with the original LinkedIn visual. Giacomo Prandelli’s post claims the top ten FPSO projects through 2030 are worth about ninety-four billion dollars. Scan the QR code to open the source post. The chart concentrates capital in Guyana, Suriname, Brazil, and one Nigerian project.

segments.title: Capital clusters across the Atlantic margin

heading.text: Capital clusters across the Atlantic margin

inlines.text: Capital clusters across the Atlantic margin

paragraph.text: Marker area is proportional to stated CAPEX; color identifies operator. Coordinates are representative offshore project positions for portfolio visualization, not navigation.

inlines.text: Marker area is proportional to stated CAPEX; color identifies operator. Coordinates are representative offshore project positions for portfolio visualization, not navigation.

entries.text: Geography sharpens the ranking's central signal. Nine projects cluster along the South Atlantic margin, from Guyana and Suriname to Brazil, while Bonga is the sole West African entry. Marker area follows stated CAPEX and color identifies the operator, revealing both geographic and operator concentration at a glance.

segments.title: What do the numbers actually include?

heading.text: What do the numbers actually include?

inlines.text: What do the numbers actually include?

widget.body:
Those values are including which investments, besides the FPSOs per se?

Luciano Jorge de Carvalho Junior · LinkedIn comment

A precise-looking ranking can still mislead if FPSO vessel prices are mixed with full-field development costs. Until primary sources define a common cost boundary, the visual’s figures are treated as stated project values — not FPSO-only CAPEX.

entries.text: A comment under the post asked the decisive question: which investments, besides the FPSOs themselves, are included in these values? That question sets the research agenda. Without a shared cost boundary, the ranking can look exact while comparing unlike scopes.

paragraph.text: Transcribe the LinkedIn ranking and ten project rows into their own provenance file. Do not rewrite the published visual.

inlines.text: Transcribe the LinkedIn ranking and ten project rows into their own provenance file. Do not rewrite the published visual.

paragraph.text: Store comment-proposed projects — Wisting, Tangkulo, Bay du Nord, Venus, PAJ — as discussion mentions, not silent inserts into the top ten.

inlines.text: Store comment-proposed projects — Wisting, Tangkulo, Bay du Nord, Venus, PAJ — as discussion mentions, not silent inserts into the top ten.

paragraph.text: Match each amount against operator, regulator, and contract disclosures from ExxonMobil, TotalEnergies, Equinor, Petrobras, ANPG, and others.

inlines.text: Match each amount against operator, regulator, and contract disclosures from ExxonMobil, TotalEnergies, Equinor, Petrobras, ANPG, and others.

paragraph.text: Label each row verified development CAPEX, identity-only, conflicting scope, or not ranked — keeping currencies and horizons intact.

inlines.text: Label each row verified development CAPEX, identity-only, conflicting scope, or not ranked — keeping currencies and horizons intact.

paragraph.text: Do not convert CAD or NOK, or promote lower-bound estimates, into a new ordinal league table without a common basis.

inlines.text: Do not convert CAD or NOK, or promote lower-bound estimates, into a new ordinal league table without a common basis.

paragraph.text: Keep original, comment, and independent values in separate fields so downstream analysis cannot silently substitute one layer for another.

inlines.text: Keep original, comment, and independent values in separate fields so downstream analysis cannot silently substitute one layer for another.

entries.text: The method is deliberate. First preserve the trigger visual. Then capture LinkedIn discussion separately. Next consult primary sources. Classify what each source actually proves. Refuse false precision when currencies or horizons do not match. Finally publish a reconciliation that keeps every evidence layer distinct.

paragraph.text: Published amounts match operator disclosures — as whole-development investment, including FPSO, not vessel-only prices. Luciano’s scope question is answered for these four.

inlines.text: Published amounts match operator disclosures — as whole-development investment, including FPSO, not vessel-only prices. Luciano’s scope question is answered for these four.

paragraph.text: Petrobras materials confirm project identity and naming (P-80 to P-85), but the visual’s CAPEX figures remain unverified. Ranks are retained provisionally.

inlines.text: Petrobras materials confirm project identity and naming (P-80 to P-85), but the visual’s CAPEX figures remain unverified. Ranks are retained provisionally.

paragraph.text: Visual shows US$8bn. Newer reporting points to roughly US$10bn CAPEX and up to US$20bn total investment including life-cycle opex. Flagged, not re-ranked.

inlines.text: Visual shows US$8bn. Newer reporting points to roughly US$10bn CAPEX and up to US$20bn total investment including life-cycle opex. Flagged, not re-ranked.

heading.text: Bay du Nord, Wisting, Greater PAJ, Tangkulo, Venus

inlines.text: Bay du Nord, Wisting, Greater PAJ, Tangkulo, Venus

paragraph.text: Comment additions expand geography, but stay NR until currency, horizon, and cost boundary are comparable to the visual’s USD top ten.

inlines.text: Comment additions expand geography, but stay NR until currency, horizon, and cost boundary are comparable to the visual’s USD top ten.

entries.text: Independent sources verify four of the original amounts — Uaru, Whiptail, GranMorgu, and Raia — as full development investments. Petrobras rows are identity-confirmed but amount-open. Bonga conflicts in scope and is flagged. Comment projects remain unranked rather than forced into the table.

entries.text: The updated ranking keeps the visual’s original order. Green marks independently matched development CAPEX. Grey marks amounts whose scope is still unresolved. Orange marks Bonga’s conflicting newer headline. Five discussion projects sit below as not ranked, with native currencies preserved.

segments.title: From industry signal to trusted perspective

heading.text: From industry signal to trusted perspective

inlines.text: From industry signal to trusted perspective

widget.body:
A single industry post became the starting point for a structured analysis of the FPSO market — combining source research, project reconciliation, cost-scope analysis, and strategic interpretation.

The result is not simply a fact check.

It is a professional industry perspective built from the evidence available.

The input can be a single industry signal.
The output is a trusted perspective on the industry.

Rapid synthesisMove from an emerging industry signal to a structured analytical view in hours, not weeks.
Evidence-led judgmentCombine public disclosures, company materials, government sources, contractor information, and industry context.
Strategic interpretationGo beyond what is stated to understand what the data reveals about capital deployment, development models, ownership, risk, and opportunity.

Exergy xFrame Insights

Turn fragmented information into a decision-quality view of the industry.

entries.text: A single industry post became the starting point for a structured FPSO market analysis. The result is not simply a fact check, but a professional industry perspective built from the available evidence. Exergy xFrame Insights combines rapid synthesis, evidence-led judgment, and strategic interpretation to turn fragmented information into a decision-quality view of the industry.
